title: Conjecture 1 (Chepoi, Estellon, and Vaxès)
statement_text: There exists a constant $c$ such that for every integer $r \geq 0$, every planar graph $G$, and every $r$-ball hypergraph $H$ of $G$, we have $\tau(H) \leq c \cdot \nu(H)$.
context_text: Chepoi, Estellon, and Vaxès conjectured this in 2007 as a generalization of their earlier result showing that planar graphs of diameter at most $2r$ can be covered by a universally bounded number of balls of radius $r$. A key aspect is that the constant $c$ must be independent of the radius $r$; if $c$ is allowed to depend on $r$ the result was already known for all graph classes with bounded expansion (Dvořák). Bousquet and Thomassé gave partial evidence by proving $\tau(H) \leq c_t \cdot \nu(H)^{2t+1}$ for $K_t$-minor-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture and a strictly stronger generalisation (Theorem 2): for every integer $t \geq 1$ there exists a constant $c_t$ such that $\tau(H) \leq c_t \cdot \nu(H)$ for every $K_t$-minor-free graph $G$ and every ball hypergraph $H$ of $G$, removing both the planarity restriction and the equal-radius restriction on the balls.
notes: The provided PDF extraction is truncated before Section 6 (the concluding section the authors mention contains a construction and likely further open problems); no additional conjectural items were recoverable from the available text.
paper_title: Packing and covering balls in graphs excluding a minor
